LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND RASIER, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S "ALLEGATIONS FOR THE PURPOSE OF PREJUDICING UBER" ARE COLORFUL, BUT WITHIN THE REALM OF ACCEPTABLE PLEADING, PARTICULARLY FOR BACKGROUND. BECAUSE THE BASIS FOR VICARIOUS LIABILITY IS ADEQUATELY PLED ON THE BASIS OF THE COMMON CARRIER ALLEGATIONS, THAT PART OF THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS ALSO DENIED, AS THE CLAIM FOR CONCEALMENT BY FRAUD IS ADEQUATELY PLED AND FRAUD PLAYS A PROMINENT ROLE IN PUNITIVE DAMAGES LAW. (SEE, E.G., CACI 3946.) ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR# 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Borna Bandari, Esq., Razavi Law Group, (949)500-1962 for Plaintiff Jane Doe
Gary Wolenski, Esq and Paul Alarcon Esq., Buchalter, APC, for Defendants Uber Technologies et al.
Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Court Reporter

Counsel for defendants, Uber Technologies contests the Court's tentative ruling.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint:

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC's motion to strike is denied. Plaintiff's "allegations for the purpose of prejudicing Uber" are colorful, but within the realm of acceptable pleading, particularly for background. Because the basis for vicarious liability is adequately pled on the basis of the common carrier allegations, that part of the motion is denied. The motion to strike punitive damages is also denied, as the claim for concealment by fraud is adequately pled and fraud plays a prominent role in punitive damages law. (See, e.g., CACI 3946.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Borna Bandari, Esq., Razavi Law Group, (949)500-1962 for Plaintiff Jane Doe
Gary Wolenski, Esq and Paul Alarcon Esq., Buchalter, APC, for Defendants Uber Technologies et al.
Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Court Reporter

Counsel for defendants, Uber Technologies contests the Court's tentative ruling.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint:

3, 5 (intentional and negligent misrepresentation). Plaintiff fails to plead fraud with the required specificity - "how, when, where, to whom and by what means." (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) This demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

4 (concealment). Less specificity is required in the context of negative fraud or "when it appears from the nature of the allegations that the defendant must necessarily possess full information concerning the facts of the controversy . . . ." (See Alfaro v. Community Housing Improvement System & Planning Assn., Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384.) This demurrer is overruled.

7-10 (assault, sexual battery, intentional infliction of emotional distress, false imprisonment). The first amended complaint ("FAC")'s allegation that "[a]s a common carrier, Uber is vicariously liable for its employees' and agents' intentional and negligent torts" is sufficiently pled for factual development. (FAC, 40:20-22.) Defendants argue that "Uber cannot be held vicariously liable for Rodas's acts, even if he was an employee or agent (which he is not), since he acted on personal motivations that were not generated by or an outgrowth of workplace responsibilities, conditions or events." (Dem., 10:24-26.) While Defendant's motion shows that some courts have sustained demurrers as to the scope of employment, the cases Defendant relies on are in different contexts, such as student/teacher and priest/parishioner. This demurrer is overruled.

5 (Ralph Act). The first amended complaint adequately pleads the elements of a Ralph Act claim as to the demurring defendants. (FAC, 35:21-36-25; CACI 3063.) This demurrer is overruled.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
